model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: SMU's significantly higher efficiency ratings, particularly a top-30 offense that should exploit Miami (OH)'s weaker defense, is the key differentiator. The Mustangs' experience against a much tougher ACC schedule has prepared them for this moment, while the RedHawks' remarkable 31-1 record was built against a far weaker MAC. Expect SMU's superior talent and athleticism to control the game and secure a win, though Miami's resilience could keep it competitive for a while.

reasoningStep.title: Evaluation of Team Quality and Context
reasoningStep.summary: A holistic review of efficiency metrics, strength of schedule, and game context strongly favors SMU, despite Miami (OH)'s impressive record.
evidence: KenPom/Torvik ratings: SMU (rank 42) vs. Miami (OH) (rank 93). SMU's offensive efficiency rank is 27 vs. Miami's 70, and defensive efficiency is notably better (104.8 vs. 108.6). SMU's 20-13 record came against a top-tier ACC schedule, while Miami's 31-1 mark was built in the MAC. No major, confirmed injury absences were found for either team that would significantly alter the projection.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

SMU has a much stronger adjusted efficiency margin (18.11 vs 8.25). SMU's offense is ranked 26/27 in adjusted offensive efficiency, while Miami's offense is 68/70. SMU's defense is ranked 95/104.8, while Miami's defense is 149/108.6. So SMU appears significantly stronger by metrics despite Miami's gaudy record.

However, Miami's 31-1 record is impressive regardless of conference. I need to consider context: MAC vs ACC competition. SMU played a much tougher schedule.
